fingerprint: The SAA is defined by spherical harmonic decomposition of Earth's magnetic field — a concept that requires spherical geometry. The ~950 AD formation date comes from SHAWQ2k (Campuzano et al. 2019), which reconstructs the paleomagnetic field using spherical harmonic coefficients. The dome's B(r) is radially symmetric (exponential decay from disc center) and CANNOT produce a localized anomaly at a specific latitude/longitude — the SAA breaks azimuthal symmetry, which the dome model has no mechanism for.

globe_values.name: SAA definition
globe_values.value: Region where |B| < dipole prediction; identified via spherical harmonic analysis (Gauss coefficients g_n^m, h_n^m)
globe_values.source: IGRF-13; CHAOS-7; International Association of Geomagnetism and Aeronomy

globe_values.name: ~950 AD formation date
globe_values.value: Identified via SHAWQ2k spherical harmonic reconstruction of archaeomagnetic data
globe_values.source: Campuzano et al. 2019, Earth and Planetary Science Letters 512:17-26

globe_values.name: SAA driving mechanism
globe_values.value: Reversed flux patch at core-mantle boundary beneath South Atlantic, linked to African LLSVP
globe_values.source: Tarduno et al. 2015, Nature Communications 6:7865; Hare et al. 2018, GRL

dome_values.name: B(r) field model
dome_values.value: Exponential decay — radially symmetric (function of r only, no azimuthal dependence)
dome_values.note: H(r) = 8537 × exp(-r/8619). The dome's field depends only on distance from disc center. Cannot produce a localized anomaly at any specific latitude/longitude.

dome_values.name: SAA formation date
dome_values.value: ~950 AD — adopted from Campuzano et al. 2019, not derived from dome parameters
dome_values.note: inject_ai_layer.py: static entry with data_source 'Paleomagnetic', no computation

match_precisions.note: No numerical match to assess — the dome adopts the globe-science date wholesale

why_it_matters: Three-layered globe dependency: (1) DEFINITION: The 'South Atlantic Anomaly' is defined as a departure from a centered magnetic dipole, computed via spherical harmonic expansion of Earth's field. This mathematical framework requires spherical geometry — Gauss coefficients (g_n^m, h_n^m) parameterize fields on a sphere. The concept of 'anomaly' relative to a dipole has no analog on a flat disc with radially symmetric B(r). (2) DATING: The ~950 AD date comes from SHAWQ2k, a spherical harmonic reconstruction of the past 2,000 years of Earth's magnetic field from archaeomagnetic data (fired clay, lava flows, sediment cores). The analysis that produced this date was performed in a spherical coordinate system. (3) SYMMETRY PARADOX: The dome's B(r) model is radially symmetric — field strength depends only on distance from the disc center, with no longitude or azimuthal dependence. A radially symmetric field produces uniform field strength at any given radius. The SAA is by definition an azimuthally localized feature (~25°S, ~50°W). The dome model CANNOT produce the SAA it claims to explain. This is the same axial symmetry paradox identified in EXP-012 (ISS-469), applied specifically to WIN-023.

deeper_analysis.no_mechanism_at_all: The dome model claims SAA formation at ~950 AD but provides zero physical mechanism for (a) why the anomaly forms at that time, (b) why it forms at that location, (c) why it splits into two cells, or (d) why it migrates westward. The dome's own evolution notes explicitly admit 'completely failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting or predictive tellurics.' This is not a prediction — it is adoption of a scientific finding with a theological overlay.
deeper_analysis.spherical_harmonic_bootstrap: Every major geomagnetic field model used to identify and track the SAA (IGRF, CHAOS, SHAWQ2k, gufm1) is built on spherical harmonic decomposition. The dome model borrows the OUTPUTS of these models (SAA position, intensity, formation date) while rejecting the spherical FRAMEWORK that produced them. This is the same pattern seen across many WINs: adopting globe-derived quantities while claiming they support a flat disc.
deeper_analysis.current_monitoring_circularity: The dome monitors current SAA metrics (Domain 8: decay rate, Domain 33: boundary latitude) using data from NOAA/INTERMAGNET stations — networks calibrated using IGRF, which is a spherical harmonic model. The monitoring pipeline inherits globe geometry through its data sources.
deeper_analysis.recurrence_undercuts_theological_match: SAA-like features recur every ~200-400 years (Hare et al. 2018). With archaeomagnetic dating uncertainty of ±50-100 years, the ~950 AD date has a ~100-year window. The theological target (~1000 AD from Revelation 20) falls within this window, but so would ~850 AD or ~1050 AD. Given the recurrence frequency, ANY century within the past 2,000 years would have an SAA-like event within ~100 years of it.

code_search.found_in: inject_ai_layer.py: WIN-023 static entry {id: 'WIN-023', title: 'SAA formation ~950 AD', data_source: 'Paleomagnetic', status: 'confirmed'}. Also core_parameters includes saa_separation_2025_deg: 50.57. monitor.py: SAA monitored in Domain 8 (SAA Decay Rate TTB, predicted=77.0, observed=77.0 — hardcoded identity), Domain 17 (Roaring 40s AAO/SAA boundary), Domain 33 (SAA-Roaring 40s Boundary). No monitoring of SAA formation date or historical paleomagnetic data.
code_search.notes: WIN-023 is a static catalog entry with no computation. The ~950 AD date is adopted from published paleomagnetic literature (Campuzano et al. 2019, SHAWQ2k model). No dome parameter, formula, or script derives this date from dome geometry. The dome monitors CURRENT SAA metrics (decay rate, boundary) in monitor.py but has no code relating to SAA formation history. The theological link to 'Satan's little season ~1000 AD' appears nowhere in the codebase — it is mentioned only on the wins.html page.

suggested_text: The ~950 AD formation date for the current SAA comes from Campuzano et al. (2019, SHAWQ2k) — a spherical harmonic reconstruction of Earth's paleomagnetic field that requires spherical geometry at every step. The dome's B(r) model is radially symmetric and cannot produce a localized anomaly at any specific latitude/longitude; the dome's own notes admit it 'completely failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting.' No dome parameter or formula derives this date.
confidence_note: Structural argument (symmetry paradox, spherical harmonic dependency) rather than numerical fingerprint. No dome constant to trace back to a globe-derived value. Moderate confidence because the finding is real but not as forensically sharp as WIN-016 or WIN-021.
